summary: After enduring injuries and an insult, Montresor secretly vows revenge against Fortunato.
During carnival, Montresor meets Fortunato and tempts him with doubts about a supposed cask of Amontillado.
Montresor has arranged for his attendants to leave the palazzo during carnival by ordering them to stay home.
Montresor leads Fortunato down into the damp catacombs while Fortunato insists on continuing despite his cough.
In the catacombs, Montresor describes his family arms and motto, then shows Fortunato a trowel when asked if he is a mason.
Montresor leads Fortunato into a recess and chains him to the granite wall.
Montresor builds a masonry wall across the recess while Fortunato sobers, struggles, screams, and pleads.
Montresor seals the last stone, restores the bones against the wall, and says no one has disturbed them for fifty years.
